calendar.matters:
Petition To Confirm Trust Title To Real Property
Off Calendar Aug-19-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for petitioner Rachael Lohrey

The Court stated that he was now satisfied regarding the validity of the schedule of assets to be provided to the trust, but noted there was ambiguous reference to the specific properties in escrow. Mr. Connors stated that as he had addressed this in his papers, the schedule referenced properties in San Francisco, and there were only two, so the intention had been adequately expredded for both of them to go into the trust. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)

Petition To Confirm Trust Title To Real Property
Off Calendar Jul-19-2016 Continued To Aug-16-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Brian Connors for and with (trustee) Rachel Danel Lohrey.

The Court stated that this is a petition to confirm certain property as trust property, that there are 2 separate groups, Morgan Hill and Olympic Valley, that in regards to Morgan Hill it is clearly listed on the document, that the issue is that the Estate Planning Schedule is not mentioned in the trust, that is raises questions as to whether the document really is part of the trust and can transfer property into the trust, that in regards to Olympic Valley the issue is whether it is designated enough in writing to be transferred, that the estate planning page only refers to other properties in escrow meaning that the property was not yet in the name of the decedent, that it questions if it is sufficiently specific to satisfy the statute of fraud, that her partner states that several properties were in escrow and that it is not entirely clear that it references this property. Mr. Connors stated that regarding Morgan Hill the trust provides for any schedule of property attached. The Court asked how is it to know that it was attached to the trust at the time of its making, that it is not labeled as exhibit A and that it is a different typeface. Mr. Connors stated that it could happen with any trust. The Court stated that it is usually very clear, that it is usually referenced and that it is usually not ambiguous. Ms. Lohrey stated that when she found the trust it was stapled to it and that the attorney who drafted it could speak to its inclusion. The Court stated that it does not have that before it now and will not accept oral testimony. Mr. Connors stated that they will submit an additional declaration, that in regards to Olympic Valley they are following the Supreme Court's long held doctrines regarding fraud, that it should be sufficient regarding the statute of fraud, that it has references to properties in escrow, that there is a deed from the LLC to the decedent as well as a declaration from her partner and cited another case. The Court stated that the law is relatively clear, that it is a question of facts in the case, that if it was still in escrow the element of ownership may be missing, that there is a discrepancy between the schedule and what her partner says and that it is not clear or sufficiently specific. Mr. Connors stated that regarding the issue of declaring intent of what was in escrow, that just because she mentioned 2 properties they did not feel it necessary to trace the second property. The Court stated that she had 7 years to clear this up and that the ambiguity is her partner declaring several properties which is more than two. Mr. Connors stated that we now have a deed from the LLC to the decedent, that the trust existed whether she did anything during the 7 years and that there is a pour over will that designates everything goes into the trust. The Court stated that the pour over will is irrelevant, that the point of Heggstad and this petition is to shortcut probate and to prove that during her life she created sufficient documents to show intent. Mr. Connors stated that the Court should also look at how this affects a third party and that there is no third party being deprived. The Petition to confirm trust title to real property was continued to August 16, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to provide additional evidence on the schedule of estate planning and or specification of Olympic Valley.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
